sourceMovie.title: The Admiral: Roaring Currents
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Admiral: Roaring Currents for the same reason it broke through in 2014: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Kim Han-min keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The war and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: My Way
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Admiral: Roaring Currents clicked for you, My Way (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in war and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Admiral: Roaring Currents clicked for you, Danger Close: The Battle of Long Tan (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in war and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Admiral: Roaring Currents?
faq.answer: Try My Way, Danger Close: The Battle of Long Tan, Greyhound—each shares DNA with The Admiral: Roaring Currents while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Admiral: Roaring Currents?
faq.answer: The Admiral: Roaring Currents (2014) is best described as War, Action, Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Admiral: Roaring Currents part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Admiral Yi Trilogy" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
